meta.title: Jaguar V12 HE Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
meta.description: Glorious V12 with cooling and ignition fragility — rated from 40+ real UK enquiries. Compare prices from 15+ vetted specialists today.

@graph.name: Jaguar models fitted with the AJ-V6 Petrol

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar AJ-V6 petrol engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ-V6 typically costs £2,500–£4,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], with variation by displacement and rebuild quality. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Older X-Type examples may not justify the expense.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar AJ-V6 petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ-V6 scores 3.5★ out of 5, with 80% confidence based on market data and specialist reports. It's a durable, smooth engine, but coolant crossover pipe leaks and timing chain tensioner wear are documented weak points on higher-mileage units.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common Jaguar AJ-V6 petrol failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant leaks from the plastic crossover pipe (rear of engine) are the most frequent failure — often causing overheating and head gasket risk. Timing chain tensioner wear is also common, typically appearing around 70,000–100,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar models use the AJ-V6 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: The naturally aspirated AJ-V6 powered the X-Type (2.5/3.0), S-Type (3.0), early XF (3.0), XJ (3.0/3.2) and some XK (3.0) models — spanning roughly 2001–2015.

mainEntity.name: How long does the Jaguar AJ-V6 petrol last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular coolant system maintenance and timely tensioner checks, the AJ-V6 can exceed 150,000 miles. Neglected cooling systems often cause failure well before that — pre-emptive replacement of the crossover pipe is strongly advised.

hero.h1: Jaguar AJ‑V6 Petrol Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJ‑V6 is a smooth, naturally‑aspirated six‑cylinder — reliable overall, but cooling system and timing chain issues are common. Backed by strong UK demand across multiple models [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: Confidence: 80% confidence — based on market data and specialist workshop consensus; proprietary enquiry volume for this specific family is not yet isolated.

rows.metric: Mix of straightforward repairs (water pumps, coil packs) and more involved ones (timing tensioners, coolant crossover pipes) — anchored to the middle point

rows.value: Well‑supported across UK Jaguar specialists; many parts available aftermarket and genuine; some specific items (e.g. 3.2 timing parts) may have lead times

rows.metric: Reconditioned supply £3,250 against the lowest host value (X‑Type ~£2,000) = 162% — well above the 75% scrap threshold

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: "The AJ‑V6 is a smooth, reliable petrol six — but cooling system seals and timing tensioners wear with age — buy only with documented recent maintenance." (Clause 1: 8 words. Clause 2: 8 words. Clause 3: 7 words. Total: 23 words.)
rows.value: Best for: Enthusiasts who value a silky V6 character and regular servicing.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an AJ‑V6 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
